what_it_does: Declares a generated desktop surface specification for view D7, titled 'Self-mod — revert latest change', binding it to journey J8 and a concrete set of ui:// addresses, and records how it is built and linted.
observations: It is a surface-spec JSON for view id D7 in area D.
Title is 'Self-mod — revert latest change' and it represents 'SM-revert'.
Platform scope is limited to desktop.
Home journey and only journey is J8; no cross_journeys are listed.
Addresses include ui://board/revert, ui://workshop/revert, ui://workshop/self-changes, and ui://workshop/self-changes/sm-revert.
Build instructions reference components 'board' and 'workshop', a design lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
standouts: The file explicitly documents that it was generated and warns against inventing addresses.
Multiple ui:// addresses point to closely related revert/self-change concepts within workshop and board.
Status already marked quality-passed despite being a generated spec.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, and build_to.
References ui:// URI addresses rather than file paths.
Separates home_journey from journeys and cross_journeys.
concerns: No cross_journeys are listed even though the address set spans board and workshop, which may or may not be intentional.
The design_lint command string mixes human annotation '(the FORM gate)' with shell syntax.
No code symbols are extracted from this file.
notes: This is the quality-passed, desktop-only surface spec for the revert-latest-change view, anchored to journey J8 and four ui:// addresses across board and workshop.
questions: Why does a single view need four distinct ui:// addresses, and which one is primary?
Are ui://board/revert and ui://workshop/revert distinct surfaces or aliases of the same D7 view?
What does 'SM-revert' represent beyond the title?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface-spec for view D7 carrying address-refs; the file matches that purpose.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to pin a specific revert-related UI view to a stable set of addresses and a build pipeline so that code generation can target the right components without inventing paths.
novelty: It embeds build-time lint and token-source metadata directly in the surface spec, and uses a multi-address ui:// binding for one view.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, lint-bound address map for the revert-latest-change view and records its journey and platform scope for downstream build and fusion queries.
summary_for_embedding: A quality-passed desktop surface spec for view D7 in area D, bound to journey J8 and four ui:// revert/self-change addresses, with build instructions targeting board and workshop components.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a stable, generated specification for the self-mod revert-latest-change view so builders know which addresses, components, and quality gates apply.
